page: Amazon Web Services r8in.96xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor architecture. It features 384 virtual CPUs mapped to 192 physical cores using Intel x86_64 processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides dedicated CPU allocation with two threads per core, paired with 3,072.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes. To support high-throughput storage and network traffic, the r8in.96xlarge offers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. The absence of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-based storage configurations for persistent data.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8in.96xlarge represents a high-density resource class designed to consolidate large-scale enterprise workloads. The combination of massive memory capacity, high-frequency Intel processors, and high-speed network interfaces makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive applications. Typical workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, big data analytics, and heavy containerized deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services r8in.96xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and Intel x86_64 processors. It delivers 384 dedicated vCPUs across 192 physical cores operating at 3.9 GHz, alongside 3,072.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but compensates with a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. Designed for resource-dense enterprise environments, the r8in.96xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency by consolidating massive datasets into memory. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and real-time data analytics.
